title: ‘Papa, they will kill me’: Newlywed teen calls dad for help, found hanging hours later in UP
content: A 19-year-old woman was found dead in her in-laws' home in Amroha, UP, hours after she reportedly pleaded with her father to rescue her, fearing for her life. Her family alleges dowry death, claiming she was murdered by her husband and in-laws after they demanded Rs 10 lakh. An FIR has been registered.

title: Vande Mataram now compulsory in Bengal madrassas: Suvendu govt passes new order
content: West Bengal's school education department has mandated the singing of 'Vande Mataram' during morning assemblies in state-run schools and madrassas, effective immediately. This directive follows a Union home ministry call to mark 150 years of the national song. Schools are seeking clarification on whether the state song will continue alongside 'Vande Mataram' and the National Anthem.

title: After Bihar poll drubbing, Prashant Kishor opts for ashram life till next polls
content: Prashant Kishor has relocated to a Patna ashram until the Bihar assembly polls, aiming for his Jan Suraaj Party to make an impact after a previous electoral setback. He criticized Chief Minister Nitish Kumar's son's cabinet induction and urged voters to prioritize their future over caste or monetary inducements, warning of potential economic challenges from West Asian conflicts.

title: 'We could have handled it better': Tamim Iqbal on Bangladesh’s T20 WC pull out
content: Former Bangladesh captain Tamim Iqbal opens up on his transition into cricket administration after a heart attack, Bangladesh’s fast bowling rise, the future of India-Bangladesh relations, the growing influence of T20 leagues. Bangladesh Cricket Board's interim president also shares his vision to restore Bangladesh cricket’s reputation, which has taken a beating after they decided to pull out from the T20 World Cup.

title: Months after tie-up with OpenAI and Anthropic, Intuit to layoff 3,000 employees
content: Financial software giant Intuit is reportedly laying off nearly 17% of its global workforce, impacting around 3,000 employees. This significant restructuring, aimed at simplifying operations and boosting AI integration, follows recent partnerships with OpenAI and Anthropic. The move comes as Intuit, like other tech firms, pivots towards AI-driven efficiencies amidst broader industry layoffs.

title: Rupee rebounds 61 paise from record low, rises to 96.25 against US dollar
content: The Indian Rupee saw a welcome rebound, gaining 61 paise to trade at 96.25 against the US dollar, driven by easing oil prices. Despite this recovery, the currency remains under pressure, marking a nine-session losing streak. The Reserve Bank of India announced a $5 billion dollar-rupee swap auction to bolster banking system liquidity amidst global uncertainties and foreign investor outflows.

title: BCCI worried whether Rohit Sharma can stay fit till 2027 ODI World Cup
content: As the ODI World Cup approaches, concerns are rising regarding Rohit Sharma and Hardik Pandya's physical condition. Despite their inclusion in the Afghanistan series, the BCCI is wary of their effectiveness in the 50-over game. The selectors are urgently seeking information on Rohit's overall fitness and Hardik's bowling readiness, emphasizing the necessity for backup opening batsmen.

title: 'Unfortunately, your role has been eliminated': Meta leadership in email to employees
content: Meta has initiated significant layoffs, impacting thousands globally as roles are eliminated to enhance efficiency and fund AI investments. Employees are informed via email about severance, visa support, and system access termination. A non-working notice period is in effect, with continued pay and benefits until the termination date. Outplacement services are also provided.

title: IPL 2026 scripts history! KKR’s win over Mumbai Indians sets an all-time record
content: IPL 2026 has set a new record with 42 successful chases, surpassing the 2016 season's mark. Kolkata Knight Riders' victory over Mumbai Indians cemented this achievement, highlighting a significant trend favoring teams batting second. This shift has sparked discussions about potential disadvantages for first-batting sides in the league.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott can’t find place in top donors list despite ‘gifting’ billions
content: Despite gifting $7.1 billion in 2025, MacKenzie Scott was excluded from the Chronicle of Philanthropy's top donor list. Her substantial donations, exceeding Jeff Bezos' lifetime giving, were overlooked due to a lack of public details about Yield Giving's operations. Scott prioritizes private, impactful giving over public recognition.

title: Heatwave alert for Delhi, UP, Punjab, Haryana and more: What to expect over next 5 days
content: Northwest India is battling severe heatwave conditions, with temperatures soaring across multiple states. Uttar Pradesh's Banda recorded a scorching 48°C, while Delhi anticipates touching 46°C. Punjab and Haryana are also reeling under intense heat. The IMD has issued alerts, advising extreme caution for vulnerable populations and recommending hydration and protective measures.

title: Could Elon Musk become world’s first trillionaire? SpaceX IPO, Mars dream & money math
content: Elon Musk is on the cusp of becoming the world's first trillionaire as SpaceX prepares for a monumental IPO, potentially the largest in Wall Street history. The company's $1.25 trillion valuation could significantly boost Musk's net worth, fueled by his majority stake and ambitious plans for AI, Starlink, and Mars colonization.

title: Ronda Rousey’s net worth 2026: Earnings, endorsements and lifestyle
content: Ronda Rousey, a trailblazer in combat sports, is projected to have a net worth of nearly $14 million by 2026. Her fortune stems from lucrative UFC and WWE careers, acting, endorsements, and book sales. Rousey's impact on women's MMA and her transition to WWE cemented her status as a crossover star, ensuring her continued relevance and earning potential.

title: Bryson DeChambeau slammed after moon landing claims, PGA flop
content: Bryson DeChambeau went viral after questioning the Apollo 11 moon landing footage during a podcast appearance. The comments came after he missed the cut at the PGA Championship. Fans heavily criticized his opinion online, while DeChambeau also opened up about possibly choosing YouTube content creation over professional golf in the future.

title: Jeff Bezos favored to buy Seahawks from Microsoft co-founder’s estate
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos is projected as the leading buyer for the Seattle Seahawks. The NFL team was owned by late former Microsoft co-founder Paul Allen. After his passing in 2018, it is now managed by her sister Jody Allen under the Paul G. Allen Trust. It is now officially up for sale. Jeff Bezos has shown interest in owning a local NFL franchise for over seven years. The sale process is expected to conclude by the 2026 offseason.

title: Mystery substance scare: 3 dead, first responders quarantined over toxic exposure in US
content: A suspected drug overdose in rural New Mexico turned deadly, claiming three lives and exposing over a dozen first responders to an unknown substance. Emergency personnel experienced severe symptoms like nausea and dizziness, leading to widespread decontamination efforts.

title: Sanju Samson eyes special milestone in CSK's must-win clash against GT
content: Chennai Super Kings batter Sanju Samson is on the cusp of a significant IPL milestone, needing just 23 runs for his second 500-plus season. Having enjoyed a stellar debut campaign with CSK in IPL 2026, Samson has been a standout performer, carrying his T20 World Cup form.

title: Delhi-NCR taxi and auto unions 3-day strike under way: All you need to know
content: Commercial vehicle drivers in Delhi-NCR launched a three-day strike demanding fare hikes due to soaring fuel costs. Unions cite financial struggles, urging immediate government action on taxi and auto fares. However, several transport associations have distanced themselves, vowing to continue services and emphasizing national interest amidst global price challenges.

title: Falta repolling: Voting under way in Bengal's high-stakes seat amid tight security
content: Falta, which falls within Abhishek Banerjee’s bastion of Diamond Harbour, reflected the intense on-ground political heat during the West Bengal Assembly polls. Apart from authorities finding attempts to tamper with footage captured by web cameras installed at several polling stations, allegations of EVM manipulation were reported at a Falta booth, eventually prompting the Election Commission to order repolling.

title: 'PM's hair was on fire': Trump & Netanyahu spar over Iran war in phone call
content: US President Trump and Israeli PM Netanyahu clashed over Iran diplomacy, with Trump favoring a deal and Netanyahu pushing for military action.  Mediators from Qatar, Pakistan, and others are working on a revised proposal, but Trump warns the window for talks is closing, while Israel remains on high alert.

title: Another military operation on cards? USS Nimitz enters Caribbean amid US-Cuba tensions
content: Amidst escalating US-Cuba tensions, the USS Nimitz strike group has entered the Caribbean. This follows President Trump's intensified rhetoric and a recent indictment of former Cuban President Raul Castro. The US is increasing pressure on Havana, citing economic hardship and past incidents, though Trump downplays fears of direct military action.

title: IPL Playoffs: With 5 matches to go, RR hold edge as PBKS and KKR chase the final spot
content: With playoffs in sight, RR, PBKS, KKR, DC, and CSK are locked in a fierce battle for the remaining spot. RR need a win, while PBKS, KKR, DC, and CSK rely on a complex web of results and their own performances. Only mathematically alive, these teams are fighting for every advantage.

title: 'Bombs its own people': India slams Pak at UNSC, flags 'long-tainted record of genocidal acts'
content: India has strongly criticized Pakistan at the UN Security Council, accusing it of a long record of violence against civilians and citing recent incidents in Afghanistan linked to Pakistani military action. India's envoy highlighted UNAMA findings of civilian casualties due to cross-border violence and airstrikes, including an attack on a hospital during Ramadan.

title: ‘All good’: Samson, Klaasen share heartfelt posts after fiery on-field exchange
content: Following a heated on-field exchange during an IPL clash, Chennai Super Kings' Sanju Samson and Sunrisers Hyderabad's Heinrich Klaasen diffused tensions with heartwarming social media messages. Their posts, expressing mutual respect and camaraderie, quickly won over fans, showcasing sportsmanship beyond the game's intensity. This gesture highlighted a positive spirit after a dramatic encounter.

title: Reward on arrest of Twisha Sharma’s absconding husband raised to Rs 30k
content: Bhopal police have increased the reward for information on absconding lawyer Samarth Singh, husband of Twisha Sharma, to Rs 30,000. Evidence suggests Singh harassed Twisha, and a lookout notice is being issued. The National Commission for Women has sought a report on the case's progress and protection for Twisha's family.

title: Sean McVay’s old claim puts Dianna Russini back in NFL spotlight
content: Sean McVay and Dianna Russini found themselves back in NFL headlines after reporter Robert Littal shared a viral claim connected to McVay’s Commanders days. The comment quickly spread across social media and sparked fresh discussion around the Rams coach’s early career in Washington. While neither McVay nor Russini addressed the rumor publicly, the story gained attention across NFL circles. The buzz also arrived during a busy offseason for McVay as he prepares the Rams for the 2026 season following recent comments about Jared Goff, Matthew Stafford and his biggest coaching regrets.

title: Hormuz chaos nears month 3: Oil prices climb as tightened supplies squeeze fuel reserves
content: Oil prices edged higher as supply disruption fears from the Iran conflict and falling US crude stockpiles emerged. Brent crude reached $105.83 and WTI $99.23, recovering from a sharp decline after US-Iran negotiation reports. Tensions around the Strait of Hormuz persist, impacting global shipments and forcing nations to tap reserves.

title: Watch: Hardik Pandya left stunned after Minz and Chahar's comedy of errors
content: Mumbai Indians' dismal IPL 2026 campaign was highlighted by a comical fielding error during their match against Kolkata Knight Riders. Skipper Hardik Pandya expressed visible frustration as Robin Minz and Deepak Chahar fumbled a simple catch, gifting Rovman Powell a crucial lifeline. This incident underscored MI's season of injuries and inconsistent performances.

title: Prince William, Tom Hanks celebrate as Aston Villa end 30-year trophy drought
content: Aston Villa clinched their first trophy in 30 years with a commanding 3-0 Europa League final victory over SC Freiburg in Istanbul.  Youri Tielemans, Emiliano Buendía, and Morgan Rogers were on target, capping a remarkable turnaround for Unai Emery's side. This triumph solidifies Emery's legendary status in the competition and brings European glory back to Villa Park after decades.

title: Double blow for TMC: Poor MLA turnout at protest against ‘bulldozer culture’
content: Trinamool Congress faces internal unease following a poor turnout of legislators at a protest and mass resignations by councillors in two municipalities.  These events fuel speculation about emerging cracks within the party, as it grapples with rebuilding its grassroots presence after the 2026 election defeat.

title: Donald Trump warns Iran of ‘nasty’ US action as negotiations enter 'final stages'
content: President Trump announced the US is in the "final stages" of Iran negotiations, warning of "nasty" actions if a deal isn't reached.  He contrasted current US military engagements with past prolonged conflicts, highlighting reduced casualties. Trump also expressed strong support for Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu, even joking about running for the position himself.

title: PM Modi to chair ministers’ meeting on governance, reforms today
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will chair a crucial meeting of the Union council of ministers on Thursday. Discussions will revolve around ministry functioning, policy outcomes, and future plans, with a focus on reforms benefiting common citizens. The meeting also addresses the West Asia crisis and its economic implications.

title: MANN+HUMMEL opens tech, innovation centre in Karnataka
content: The new facility is designed to accelerate global product development and customer-centric innovation by integrating advanced research labs, testing infrastructure, digital engineering, and data analytics under one roof.

title: T.N. Cabinet expansion LIVE: Congress set to join Tamil Nadu’s first coalition government in decades
content: TVK top leader and Minister for Public Works and Sports Development, Aadhav Arjuna on Wednesday, May 20, urged the Congress, VCK and IUML to join the government and said it was the wish of Chief Minister Vijay.

title: After High Court verdict, Hindu petitioners seek free entry for devotees at Bhojshala in Madhya Pradesh
content: Petitioner Ashish Goyal, associated with the 'Hindu Front for Justice', in his application to the ASI on Wednesday (May 20, 2026), said worshippers should not be charged even the existing Re 1 entry fee.

title: Stray dog euthanasia: Bengaluru civic bodies examining legally permissible options for ‘rabid, incurably ill or demonstrably dangerous or aggressive dogs’
content: The Supreme Court underscored that the authorities concerned must undertake euthanasia only after due assessment by qualified veterinary experts and strictly in accordance with applicable statutory protocols

title: Congress has pushed Karnataka from revenue surplus to deficit, alleges Capt. Brijesh Chowta
content: Only achievement of Siddaramaiah government during past three years is to push progressive Karnataka to the debt trap, says MP

title: TVK-led Tamil Nadu government: full list of Ministers
content: The specific portfolios for the nine Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) ministers were formally announced on Saturday (May 16, 2026), with Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay retaining key departments, including the Home and Police.

title: U.P. CM Adityanath conducts review of Data Centre Cluster, Project Ganga and wheat processing policy
content: The Chief Minister said that the data centre cluster will create the basic infrastructure for Uttar Pradesh’s AI mission

title: Supreme Court suspects Karnataka government of ‘delaying tactics’ but extends Bengaluru civic polls’ deadline to August 31
content: The apex court said this would be the State government’s ‘last chance’, and there would be no further extensions

title: PM Modi leaves for home after concluding five-nation tour
content: PM Modi concludes a five-nation tour, enhancing strategic partnerships and cooperation with Italy, UAE, Netherlands, Sweden, and Norway

title: Kerala Assembly: MLA-designates arrive for swearing-in on foot, bicycle, vehicles
content: Congress MLA-elect from Puthuppally Chandy Oommen, son of former Kerala CM Oommen Chandy, arrived on a bicycle wearing a shirt with an image of his father on it

title: India calls out Pakistan’s ‘long-tainted record of genocidal acts’ at UNSC
content: Harish Parvathaneni's remarks came after Pakistan’s representative raised the issue of Jammu and Kashmir at the debate.

title: Voting begins for re-polling in Falta Assembly seat in West Bengal
content: Polling for the Falta Assembly seat was held in the second phase of the West Bengal Assembly elections on April 29; after large-scale irregularities were reported, the Election Commission ordered re-polling in the constituency on May 21.

title: Defeat of delimitation Bill is south India’s loss: Nara Lokesh
content: The vote against Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill has placed southern States at a disadvantage — Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Telangana and Andhra Pradesh stand to lose representation after the next Census, Telugu Desam Party’s newly appointed working president says, blaming politics, not policy for the Bill’s failure; he addresses concerns over upcoming resource-intensive data centres and explains why his government is incentivising larger families

title: No double standards, we condemn Russia, U.S. and Israel for international law violations: Norway’s Foreign Minister
content: In an interview after Modi-Store bilateral, Norwegian FM Eide says India and Norway must resist over-dependence on global powers for trade or technology

title: Modi in Norway highlights: PM holds interaction with Norwegian business leaders
content: PM Modi was conferred with Norway's highest civilian honour, the Grand Cross of the Royal Norwegian Order of Merit

title: MSME Growth Conclave in Vijayawada on May 25
content: The initiative will be unveiled during the ‘MSME Growth Conclave’ scheduled to be held at Dr. B.R. Ambedkar Kala Vedika in Vijayawada on May 25.

title: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio to start first India visit on May 23
content: Meeting comes amidst U.S.-Iran war that has hurt India’s energy security; trade and defence cooperation also on the agenda; U.S. Embassy notes that Quad ministerial meetings also expected soon

title: India and Italy have been in continuous contact over Ukraine and West Asia conflicts: PM Modi
content: Modi describes India-Italy relation as a ‘win-win partnership’ and welcomes the upgradation of bilateral ties to the level of ‘Special Strategic Partnership’

title: BJP government snatching reservation of PDA in U.P., alleges Akhilesh
content: Akhilesh Yadav, who released a ‘Samajwadi Audit Report’ regarding the misappropriation of reservation quotas, alleged that the scams and corruption have plagued recruitment processes under the BJP government

title: Bihar cabinet approves ₹10.40 cr VGF to operate direct flight between Gaya Ji-Bangkok
content: Bihar cabinet also approved ₹747.97 cr from the Bihar Contingency Fund to improve health infrastructure in rural local bodies. Nod for private investment proposals

title: Trinamool MLAs hold protest in West Bengal Assembly over hawker eviction and torture against party workers
content: Senior leaders like Firhad Hakim, Kunal Ghosh, Ritabrata Banerjee, and Nayana Bandyopadhyay were seen leading the sit-in demonstration inside the Assembly compound on May 20

title: Modi, Shah are traitors who are attacking the Constitution, says Rahul at Rae Bareli meet
content: Congress leader says that the BJP government is trying to do away with reservation given for marginalised sections

title: PM busy handing out candies in Italy amid economic storm at home, says Congress
content: Rahul Gandhi called the reel that shows Prime Minister Narendra Modi handing over a packet of toffees to Italy’s Giorgia Meloni “an insult to the people of the country”

title: PIL plea filed against deposit of temple funds in State-owned non banking finance corporations
content: Justices G.R. Swaminathan and V. Lakshminarayanan admit the plea and insist on filing of counter affidavit within a week

title: Seven Irular families rescued from brick kiln in Arani
content: Revenue officials said that the rescued families were forced to stay in small huts without proper access to drinking water and no toilet facility

title: RPF seizes 16 kg ganja at Jolarpet railway station
content: The team searched coaches of the express train where they found two abandoned tourist bags in the luggage carrier of the general compartment in the train

title: Karnataka draws up plan to supply 2.5 tmcft of Cauvery water from Kollegal to Bengaluru’s industrial areas
content: Large and Medium Industries Minister M.B. Patil says the Cauvery Water Disputes Tribunal had allocated 4 tmcft of water for industrial purposes; of this, only 1.37 tmcft is being utilised

title: Forest-dwelling tribes push for direct recruitment
content: The government had announced that 12 tribes residing on the forest fringes would be given jobs in the civil services through a special direct recruitment

title: Slew of road reforms planned for ULBs in A.P., standardised road lane markings made mandatory
content: All ULBs have been told to ensure edge lines, stop lines and zebra crossings, black-and-yellow kerb painting on roads, and cautionary traffic signage and rectification of minor road defects

title: Twisha Sharma death: NCW seeks action taken report from Madhya Pradesh government
content: Details sought regarding FIR provisions, status of accused persons’ arrest and interrogation, efforts to arrest absconding husband, passport impoundment status, CCTV footage, call records, electronic and forensic evidence, post-mortem report and action taken on any prior complaints

title: Patient injured after ceiling portion collapses at Guntur GGH
content: Preliminary assessment suggests that water leakage and rat infestation had damaged the ceiling sheet, leading to its collapse, says GGH Superintendent

title: CPI seeks legal guidelines for regulating online medicine sales
content: CPI demands that the government strengthen Drug Control Administration and State Pharmacy Council, introduce strict digital monitoring systems and allow qualified pharmacists to set up independent community pharmacy consultation clinics

title: CPI(M) opposes population incentive policy of A.P. government
content: Encouraging larger families without improving living standards will push the society into poverty and inequality, says CPI(M) State secretary

title: Supreme Court inclined to grant bail to 2020 Delhi riots accused Tasleem Ahmed and Abdul Khalid Saifi
content: Court says it is prima facie inclined to grant bail to 2020 Delhi riots accused Tasleem Ahmed and Abdul Khalid Saifi; ASG argues that conflicting views on bail in UAPA cases need to be settled by a larger Bench

title: Centre tells Supreme Court no new hydel projects should come up in upper Ganga
content: Three Union Ministries submit common affidavit in top court saying the government is not in favour of permitting any new projects in the Alaknanda and Bhagirathi river basins in Uttarakhand, apart from the seven already commissioned

title: Welfare and safety of workers top on govt. agenda, asserts Labour Minister Subhash
content: The NDA government has restored the schemes discontinued by the previous YSRCP dispensation, while creating an industry-friendly atmosphere, he says

title: Eviction attempt triggers high tension at Dalit settlement in Kunnathunadu
content: The move follows a Supreme Court verdict four years ago in favour of a private individual in a land dispute involving 2.65 acres allegedly falling within 19.30 acres of puramboke land

title: "No Faith, No Law, No Morality": India Slams Pak's 'Genocidal Acts' At UNSC
content: The Indian envoy said that such heinous acts of aggression by Pakistan should not come as a surprise from a country that "bombs its own people and conducts systematic genocide".

title: Abused, Unpaid, Children Among 400 Workers 'Bonded' At Telangana Factories
content: One rescued labourer from Sivakasi in Tamil Nadu alleged that he had been working at the kiln for nearly four years without receiving actual wages.

title: PM Modi To Chair Council Of Ministers Meet Today Amid Cabinet Reshuffle Buzz
content: The meeting will be held at 5 PM and will be attended by all cabinet ministers, ministers of state with independent charge and other ministers of state.

title: Netanyahu's 'Mistake' Warning To Trump In Hour-Long Tense Call On Iran War
content: Despite disagreement with Netanyahu, Trump maintained a united front in public about their Iran plan. He told reporters that Netanyahu "will do whatever I want him to do" on Iran, though he also said they had a good relationship.

title: 'Find Work In 60 Days Or Leave US': Layoffs Put Indian Techies In A Pickle
content: Nearly half of Indian professionals in US would consider returning to India if they lost jobs. Others looking at Canada and Europe as alternatives.

title: Parle Top Official "Thrilled" With PM Modi's 'Melody' Diplomacy In Italy
content: Parle Products plan to scale up the global presence of Melody and take it to countries other than the existing 100.

title: Telangana Engineering Student Forces Classmate To Drink Alcohol, Rapes Her
content: The accused, Uday Kumar, and the survivor are students of a prominent engineering college in Telangana's Ibrahimpatnam area.

title: "Always Sad To Say Goodbye": Mark Zuckerberg To 8,000 Laid-Off Meta Employees
content: The layoff notifications went out beginning in the early morning hours, with Singapore-based Meta workers among the first to be informed.

title: Severe Heatwave Grips Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Temperatures Cross 47 Degrees
content: Telangana and Andhra Pradesh face a severe heatwave with temperatures soaring above 44°C across multiple districts.

title: Delhi Taxi, Auto Unions Begin 3-Day Strike, Services Likely To Be Hit
content: More than 68 transport unions under the banner of the All India Motor Transport Congress (AIMTC) and the United Front of All Transport Associations (UFTA) are expected to participate in the "chakka jam" protest.

title: Retired Navy Captain Under CBI Scanner For Assets Over Rs 3 Crore
content: CBI files case against retired Navy Captain Raminder Singh Wadhwa for unexplained wealth amassed from 2010 to 2020

title: 20-Year-Old Gang Rape Survivor Dies By Suicide Over Blackmail: Cops
content: The family alleged that the accused gang-raped and filmed the crime. Following this, she was continuously blackmailed with threats of making the video viral.

title: UK Eases Sanctions On Russian Fuel Imports Amid Middle East Tensions
content: The UK had announced in October it would ban imports derived from Russian crude, part of a push to cut off revenues funding Russia's war in Ukraine.

title: Caps, Cold Water, ORS: Delhi's Heat Relief Vans See Rush As City Sizzles At 46 Degrees
content: The relief vans have been deployed across all 13 districts of Delhi under the government's Heat Action Plan 2026 and are operating daily during peak heat hours from 11 am to 6 pm.

title: 3 Cops Injured After Stones Thrown During Mumbai Anti-Encroachment Drive
content: At least ten persons were detained for stone-pelting, they said, adding that police resorted to lathi-charge to control the unruly mob, in which some residents were also injured.

title: Animal lovers can protect strays if they take responsibility for bites: SC
content: a bench of justices said the right to protect stray dogs in public spaces cannot be divorced from the obligation to ensure that such actions do not result in harm to others . the supreme court said they can feed and take care of canines, but will have to face tortious liability for any injury caused to the public .

title: NEET-UG re-examination: Bolster system in 20 days for secure test, govt tells NTA
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. title: 7 cops injured in stone-pelting during anti-encroachment drive in Mumbai slum
content: Manthan Mehta has been a journalist since 1997, and is currently Assistant Editor at The Times of India, Mumbai. He covers a wide range of subjects ranging from insurance to local crimes and civic issues. Manthan reads and watches cricket matches in his free time. Read More

title: Rupee hits 100/$ in 1-year forward, closes at 96.8 in spot trade
content: a one-year outright forward spot rupee transaction is a foreign-exchange contract where you agree the exchange rate for a USD/INR trade that will settle one year later . the premium on the dollar opened at 321 paise and rose to a high of 322 . rupee prevented from breaching 97 level DBS treasury head .

content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most."
